summary: Matched the Peach explosion item unit by replacing placeholder declarations with real C implementations for spawn/setup/update behavior, adding the item state table, tightening the public header prototypes, and flipping `itpeachexplode.c` from `NonMatching` to `Matching` in `configure.py`.

key_files.role: Added matched implementations for the Peach explosion item functions and defined `it_803F7488` item state table.
Replaced placeholder/unknown prototypes and legacy include guard with typed declarations and `ITPEACHEXPLODE_H` guard.

what_changed: `itpeachexplode.c` now includes its own header plus item/db dependencies instead of placeholder headers.
Defined `ItemStateTable it_803F7488[]` with two states, both using `it_802BD2EC` as the callback and `NULL` for the remaining handlers.

Implemented `it_802BD158` as a spawning helper returning `Item_GObj*`; it builds a `SpawnItem` for `It_Kind_Peach_Explode`, copies the input position to `prev_pos` and `pos`, assigns facing direction, zeroes damage and velocity, stores the parent gobj in both parent fields, sets `x44_flag.b0`, calls `Item_80268B18`, and on success calls `it_802BD248` and `db_80225DD8` before returning the spawned item.

Implemented `it_802BD248` as item initialization/setup: assigns `owner`, sets `xD44_lifeTimer` to `60.0F`, clears `xDAC_itcmd_var0`, runs several item setup helpers, selects animation state 1 or 0 via `Item_80268E5C` depending on the bool argument, then calls `it_80274574`.
Implemented `it_802BD2EC` as a bool wrapper around `it_802751D8(item_gobj)`.
Updated the header prototypes from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` and loose `int` usage to typed signatures using `Item_GObj*`, `Vec3*`, `bool`, and `float`.
Moved the `it_803F7488` extern declaration near the top of the header and made it explicitly `extern struct ItemStateTable it_803F7488[]`.
Renamed the header guard from address-derived `GALE01_2BD158` to file-derived `ITPEACHEXPLODE_H`.
smart_moves: The spawn function mirrors common item creation patterns by fully initializing the `SpawnItem` stack struct before calling `Item_80268B18`, including duplicated parent gobj fields and explicit zero velocity.
The setup function isolates post-spawn initialization into `it_802BD248`, letting the spawn function handle allocation and registration while setup handles owner/lifetime/animation state.
The bool argument was propagated through the typed signature and used only to choose between animation state 0 and 1, avoiding an untyped `int` placeholder.
The PR updated the header at the same time as the source, replacing unknown prototypes with usable function signatures for downstream callers.
The object was only marked `Matching` after adding real implementations and the state table, keeping build configuration aligned with decomp status.
decomp_lessons: For item spawn routines, look for a `SpawnItem` local initialized with kind, position, facing direction, parent gobj fields, damage, velocity, and flag bits before `Item_80268B18`.
Small item update callbacks may simply forward to shared item helpers; here `it_802BD2EC` returns `it_802751D8(item_gobj)`.
When matching item setup code, the order of item helper calls matters: this PR preserves a specific sequence after assigning owner/lifetime/itcmd fields.
Replace `UNK_RET` and `UNK_PARAMS` once call sites and behavior reveal exact types; doing so can also expose return-value usage such as `Item_GObj*` from a spawn helper.
Address-derived include guards such as `GALE01_2BD158` should be normalized to file-based guards when cleaning up matched item headers.
naming_conventions: Use file-based header guards like `ITPEACHEXPLODE_H` for item headers.
Use typed parameter names when known, e.g. `pos`, `dir`, `owner`, and `ref_gobj`, rather than anonymous or unknown placeholder parameters.
Keep existing doldecomp symbol names for not-yet-renamed functions and data, such as `it_802BD158`, `it_802BD248`, `it_802BD2EC`, and `it_803F7488`.
Use `Item_GObj*` for item object pointers and `Item* ip = GET_ITEM(item_gobj)` for item data access.
Use enum-style item kind names such as `It_Kind_Peach_Explode` in spawn setup.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Stack-allocate `SpawnItem spawn` and assign fields directly rather than relying on broad memset-style initialization in the shown match.
Copy `*pos` into `spawn.prev_pos`, then assign `spawn.pos = spawn.prev_pos` to match the apparent original data movement.
Use chained zero assignment for velocity components: `spawn.vel.x = spawn.vel.y = spawn.vel.z = 0.0F`.
Set `spawn.x0_parent_gobj = gobj` and then `spawn.x4_parent_gobj2 = spawn.x0_parent_gobj`, preserving dependency/order instead of assigning both independently.
Return early from the successful spawn branch after setup and debug registration, with a final `return NULL` for allocation failure.
Implement the state-table callbacks with explicit `NULL` entries and simple integer state IDs, matching the two-entry table layout.

review_feedback.note: The PR context reports no review comments and provides no human text excerpt, so there is no evidence of reviewer-requested changes.

follow_up_queries: Find other item spawn helpers using `SpawnItem` plus `Item_80268B18` to reuse the field initialization/order pattern.
Search for remaining headers with `UNK_RET`, `UNK_PARAMS`, or address-derived `GALE01_` include guards in `src/melee/it/items`.
Compare Peach item files such as `itpeachturnip.c`, `itpeachparasol.c`, and `itpeachtoad.c` for similar owner/lifetime/animation setup conventions.
Search for state tables with two entries forwarding to a shared update callback like `it_802751D8`.
